name: Grade 11 Language Arts — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 11 pathway for advanced argument, research, rhetoric, citation, and scholarly discussion. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 11 knowledge and transferable skills in advanced argument, research, rhetoric, citation, and scholarly discussion.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about advanced argument, research, rhetoric, citation, and scholarly discussion and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

units.title: Investigate: advanced argument, research, rhetoric, citation, and scholarly discussion
units.essentialQuestion: What question and evidence can guide our study of advanced argument, research, rhetoric, citation, and scholarly discussion?
outcomes: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about advanced argument, research, rhetoric, citation, and scholarly discussion.
I can use appropriate disciplinary vocabulary, evidence, and tools to communicate my reasoning.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about advanced argument, research, rhetoric, citation, and scholarly discussion, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 11 task connected to advanced argument, research, rhetoric, citation, and scholarly discussion.
The learner uses relevant evidence, vocabulary, or a model to explain reasoning.
The learner revises, seeks feedback, or identifies a specific next step.
